title: Parallel Tool Execution
constraints: All three city weather lookups must execute in parallel, not sequentially
The total execution time must be roughly that of one lookup, not three
The agent must collect and present all results together
No changes to the weather tool's internal logic
hints: In LangChain, the LLM can emit multiple tool calls in a single response if prompted to fetch all cities at once
In LangGraph, use a fan-out/fan-in pattern with parallel branches in the graph
Make sure the agent prompt encourages fetching all cities simultaneously rather than one at a time

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.tools import tool
from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
import time

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

@tool
def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    """Get current weather for a city."""
    time.sleep(1)  # Simulates API latency
    weather_data = {
        "new york": "72°F, Sunny",
        "london": "58°F, Cloudy",
        "tokyo": "68°F, Partly Cloudy",
    }
    return weather_data.get(city.lower(), f"Weather data not available for {city}")

# BUG: The agent calls get_weather sequentially for each city
# TODO: Build a LangGraph workflow that fans out weather lookups in parallel
agent = create_react_agent(llm, [get_weather])

start = time.time()
result = agent.invoke({"messages": [("human", "What's the weather in New York, London, and Tokyo?")]})
print(f"Time: {time.time() - start:.1f}s")
print(result["messages"][-1].content)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai.tools import tool
import time

@tool
def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    """Get current weather for a city."""
    time.sleep(1)
    weather_data = {
        "new york": "72°F, Sunny",
        "london": "58°F, Cloudy",
        "tokyo": "68°F, Partly Cloudy",
    }
    return weather_data.get(city.lower(), f"Weather data not available for {city}")

# BUG: The agent calls get_weather sequentially for each city
weather_agent = Agent(
    role="Weather Reporter",
    goal="Get weather for requested cities",
    backstory="You are a weather assistant.",
    tools=[get_weather],
)

task = Task(
    description="Get the current weather in New York, London, and Tokyo. Look up each city one at a time.",
    expected_output="Weather for all three cities",
    agent=weather_agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[weather_agent], tasks=[task])

start = time.time()
result = crew.kickoff()
print(f"Time: {time.time() - start:.1f}s")
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool
import time

@function_tool
def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    """Get current weather for a city."""
    time.sleep(1)
    weather_data = {
        "new york": "72°F, Sunny",
        "london": "58°F, Cloudy",
        "tokyo": "68°F, Partly Cloudy",
    }
    return weather_data.get(city.lower(), f"Weather data not available for {city}")

# BUG: The agent calls get_weather sequentially for each city
agent = Agent(
    name="Weather Reporter",
    instructions="You are a weather assistant. Get the weather for each city requested.",
    tools=[get_weather],
)

start = time.time()
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "What's the weather in New York, London, and Tokyo?")
print(f"Time: {time.time() - start:.1f}s")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Weather lookups run in parallel
evaluationCriteria.description: All three city lookups execute concurrently rather than one after another

evaluationCriteria.label: Total time is roughly one lookup
evaluationCriteria.description: The wall-clock time is approximately 1 second, not 3 seconds

evaluationCriteria.label: All city results are returned
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent presents weather data for all three cities in a single response
